title: Case study: comparing selection methods on one dataset
key_concepts: Applies filter, wrapper, and embedded methods to a single high-dimensional dataset containing relevant features, redundant features, and pure noise.
Comparing the outcomes: Which method successfully filtered out the noise? Which method efficiently handled redundancy? Which was fastest to execute?
Highlighting the trade-off between computational cost (filter is fastest, wrapper is slowest) and subset optimality.
The ultimate goal of feature selection is balancing model accuracy with simplicity and interpretability.
examples: Discuss the results conceptually: perhaps mutual information kept redundant features, while LASSO elegantly selected just one feature from a highly correlated redundant group.
code_snippets: [KODE: pipeline membandingkan filter, wrapper, dan embedded; sumber: notebook bab]
